commit.message:
perf: eliminate async overhead and reduce GC pressure (~49% faster)

- Add ISyncPacketSource interface for synchronous packet consumption
- Refactor TimestampMergedReplayer to synchronous (no Channel<T>)
- Add FeedHandler.RunSyncLoop for zero-async-overhead processing
- Use ArrayPool<byte> in PcapReader (reduce 52M allocations)
- Use 1MB BufferedStream in PcapReader for batched I/O
- Track and return pooled arrays in TimestampMergedReplayer lifecycle
- Deep-copy packet data before queuing in FeedHandler (ArrayPool safety)
- Fix race condition in PrintBookSample (try/catch InvalidOperationException)
- Add [Conditional("DEBUG")] AssertValid to BookSide

Benchmark: 52M packets processed in ~30s (was ~59s before optimizations).

commit.message:
fix: non-blocking partition DDL to prevent INSERT cascade stalls

Use SET LOCAL lock_timeout='0' in ensure_partition() so the CREATE TABLE
PARTITION OF command never queues an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock when concurrent
INSERTs are active.

Previously, any busy CZDS/CertStream worker holding a ROW EXCLUSIVE lock
(e.g. a long-running trigram similarity scan) would cause the DDL to queue
indefinitely, blocking *all* subsequent INSERT batches for the parent domain
table until the DDL completed or was manually cancelled.

With lock_timeout='0':
- DDL fails immediately if lock unavailable (no queue, no cascade)
- Session is rolled back cleanly (fixes InFailedSqlTransaction on advisory unlock)
- Re-checks pg_class after rollback: if another process created the partition
  concurrently, proceeds normally
- TLD is retried on the next ingest cycle (acceptable behaviour)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(search,wal): parallelize ANN search_batch + increase WAL BufWriter to 4 MB

- search_batch() uses par_iter for ANN path (1.46x speedup on batch=8, 16-core
  machine); brute-force path keeps sequential iter since each query already
  saturates the thread pool via internal par_chunks(512)
- WAL BufWriter capacity increased from default 8 KB to 4 MB, reducing system
  calls per append_batch from ~5000 to ~8 for 1536-dim entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add 'When to use brute-force vs. ANN' section after benchmark tables:
  explains that d<=256 favors brute-force (ANN loses 22 pp recall on GloVe-200),
  while d>=1536 favors ANN (3-5x latency gain with <3% recall cost)
- Add 'Release Comparison' section with v0.4.0 as the tracking baseline;
  from v0.5.0 onward each release row will include delta vs prior version
- Update benchmark tables and perf history from fresh full run (v0.5 branch state)

Apply WAL entries to live state in order so delete-then-reinsert keeps the latest slot across flush and reopen. Preserve metadata during live compaction and commit the QA regression tests that now pass.

* docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

over-subscribing the thread pool. Batch throughput improves for all N; Rayon
prevents deadlock and manages core sharing automatically.

commit.message:
Rewrite: the duck is silent — true rubber duck debugging

The original rubber duck method (The Pragmatic Programmer, 1999) is about
self-explanation to a silent, inanimate audience. The duck doesn't ask
questions, give feedback, or suggest fixes. The developer does all the
talking. Bugs reveal themselves mid-explanation.

Previous version incorrectly made the duck an interactive questioner
(Socratic method / pair programming). This rewrite corrects the approach:

- Skill: agent explains TO the silent duck, catches own discrepancies
- Agent: the duck is inanimate — exists as audience, not interrogator
- Output format preserved: Five Quacks session with structured report
- Added cognitive science references (self-explanation effect, cognitive
  offloading, psychological distance, metacognition)
- Added references section (Pragmatic Programmer, Coding Horror, Byrd 2023)

commit.message:
Fix RE Tape Echo + SpaceyDelayer silence, tame ShimmerReverb + MoogFilters

Root cause for RE Tape Echo and SpaceyDelayer silence:
Emscripten 4.x no longer auto-exports HEAPF32 on the module object.
Both worklets relied on module.HEAPF32 to create Float32Array buffer
views for copying audio data to/from WASM memory. With HEAPF32=undefined,
updateBufferViews() returned early, buffer views stayed null, and the
worklet process() silently returned without writing any output.

Fix: Rewrite both worklets with WebAssembly.instantiate interception
pattern (same as SpringReverb/MVerb/KissOfShame worklets) to capture
the WASM Memory object during module initialization.

ShimmerReverb was still too extreme:
- Tighten decay range: 0-1 → 0.3-0.75 (was 0.5-0.995)
- Scale shimmer feedback by 0.6x to prevent energy buildup
- tanhf soft clip on feedback + output (from previous commit)

MoogFilters was over-distorting:
- Add tanhf soft clip on filter output before wet/dry mix
- Apply drive as input gain before filter (was unused except Hyperion)
- Prevents resonance self-oscillation from clipping

Add Auth Tab Switch Browser feature flag and telemetry constants to common4j, Fixes AB#3561423 (#3072)

Adds the feature flag and attribute constants needed to instrument the
Auth Tab switch browser feature.

## Changes

### `CommonFlight.java`
- New flight:
`ENABLE_AUTH_TAB_FOR_SWITCH_BROWSER("EnableAuthTabForSwitchBrowser",
false)`

### `AttributeName.java`
Four new attributes adjacent to existing switch browser attributes:

| Constant | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth_tab_used` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab was used |
| `auth_tab_result_code` | integer | Android Activity result code from
Auth Tab |
| `is_auth_tab_supported` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab is supported |
| `auth_tab_fallback_to_custom_tabs` | boolean | Whether Auth Tab fell
back to Custom Tabs |

> **Note:** Per `AttributeName.java` policy, the four new attribute
constants must also be mirrored in the Broker repo's
(`AzureAD/ad-accounts-for-android`) `AttributeName.java`.

Auth Tab telemetry attributes will be recorded on the existing
`SwitchBrowserProcess` span via span context propagation, rather than a
dedicated new span.

fix: replace hardcoded endpoints with configurable voxquieta.org domain (#392)

* fix: replace hardcoded endpoints with configurable voxquieta.org domain

The Android release build was pointing to a raw Azure Container Apps URL
(bible-app-backend.agreeablesea-6ee07535...) instead of a proper domain.
The same URL was hardcoded in CI workflows and Makefile. Production
frontend domain (getinspiredbythebible.ai4you.sh) was hardcoded in CORS,
access audit middleware, Llama Guard referer, Turnstile WebView, and
contact emails across 11 locale files and frontend components.

Changes:
- Add configurable `production_frontend_url` setting (env: PRODUCTION_FRONTEND_URL)
  used by CORS, access audit middleware, and Llama Guard referer
- Android: update release BASE_URL default to api.voxquieta.org
- Android: update PRIVACY_POLICY_URL to voxquieta.org/privacy
- Android: fix TurnstileWebView hardcoded origin
- CI: replace 3 hardcoded Azure URLs with ${{ vars.BACKEND_API_URL }}
- CI: replace hardcoded frontend URL with ${{ vars.FRONTEND_URL }}
- Frontend: update contact email to contact@voxquieta.org in all 11 locales
- Frontend: update ContactForm.tsx and ErrorBoundary.tsx email references
- Backend: update email defaults (noreply@voxquieta.org, contact@voxquieta.org)
- Terraform: update domain and email config in tfvars and variables
- Makefile: update prod URLs and build commands
- Tests: update all URL assertions to match new domain

GitHub repo variables needed: BACKEND_API_URL, FRONTEND_URL, PRIVACY_POLICY_URL

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PxUGBRzdMn2uumrXMmhQsu

* fix: add CI fallback defaults and empty-string guard for Gradle properties

Prevents CI failures when GitHub repository variables (BACKEND_API_URL,
FRONTEND_URL) are not yet configured:

- CI workflows: add `|| 'default'` fallback in GitHub Actions expressions
  so builds use voxquieta.org defaults when vars are unset
- build.gradle.kts: add gradleProp() helper that treats blank/empty
  strings as absent, ensuring the Kotlin `?:` fallback works correctly
  even when Gradle receives `-PbaseUrl=` (empty value from CI)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PxUGBRzdMn2uumrXMmhQsu

* fix(terraform): bind api.voxquieta.org to backend Container App

Add the missing custom_domain_backend line to terraform.tfvars so that
the backend_custom_domain and backend_ssl_cert_bind null_resources in
main.tf actually provision the hostname. Without this, the Android
release build (which now defaults to https://api.voxquieta.org/) would
have nothing to reach in production.

The Cloudflare Origin Certificate uploaded via the CLOUDFLARE_ORIGIN_CERT_B64
secret is reused for the backend bind through main.tf:840's fallback to
cloudflare_origin_cert_frontend, so a single cert covering both
voxquieta.org and api.voxquieta.org (apex + wildcard SANs) is sufficient.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01PxUGBRzdMn2uumrXMmhQsu

* fix: normalize non-Latin verse.book to English in isVerseReferenced

When a Hindi/Korean/Arabic/Chinese/Russian Bible translation returns
verse.book in its localized script (e.g. "फिलिप्पियों"), the old
isVerseReferenced() compared it directly against the English strings in
the references Set (e.g. "Philippians 4:7"), causing a silent miss —
verses were never highlighted or shown in the referenced-verses panel.

Fix: apply normalizeBookName() to verse.book before all lookups and
fuzzy comparisons, so non-Latin book names are resolved to their
lowercase English canonical form (via LOCALIZED_BOOK_TO_ENGLISH) before
matching. Add regression tests for Hindi, Korean, Arabic, Chinese and
Russian to prevent future regressions.

* fix: normalize non-Latin verse.book to English in isVerseReferenced

When a Hindi/Korean/Arabic/Chinese/Russian Bible translation returns
verse.book in its localized script (e.g. "फिलिप्पियों"), the old
isVerseReferenced() compared it directly against the English strings in
the references Set (e.g. "Philippians 4:7"), causing a silent miss —
verses were never highlighted or shown in the referenced-verses panel.

Fix: apply normalizeBookName() to verse.book before all lookups and
fuzzy comparisons, so non-Latin book names are resolved to their
lowercase English canonical form (via LOCALIZED_BOOK_TO_ENGLISH) before
matching. Add regression tests for Hindi, Korean, Arabic, Chinese and
Russian to prevent future regressions.

* fix(verse-detection): Hindi edge cases — alt spelling + greedy numbered-prefix (#403)

Two distinct bugs prevented Hindi verses from being detected in LLM output like
'लेवियतियुस 1:3-4 और इब्रानियों 9:22':

1. Alternate transliteration 'लेवियतियुस' (Leviticus) was not in the Hindi
   book-name map (IRV only uses 'लैव्यव्यवस्था'), and the generic fallback
   branch already excludes Devanagari script, so unknown Hindi spellings could
   never match. Added the alias in both the frontend map and a new HINDI_ALIASES
   dict in the backend registry.

2. The numbered-prefix branch (\d+ + letters) greedily captured '4 और इब्रानियों'
   starting from the dangling '4' in the verse range '1:3-4', shadowing the real
   इब्रानियों 9:22 match. Fixed by excluding Han/Hangul/Devanagari from the letter
   classes inside the numbered-prefix branch — legitimate non-Latin numbered books
   (e.g. '1 शमूएल', '2 राजाओं') are now listed explicitly in the multi-word
   alternation instead, which is what that branch exists for anyway.

Fix ShimmerReverb: tanhf soft clip on feedback + output, tame defaults

The feedback loop had no gain limiting — pitch-shifted signal accumulated
in the tank causing runaway distortion at moderate-to-high decay/shimmer.

Changes:
- Add tanhf() soft clip on feedback path (prevents energy buildup)
- Add tanhf() soft clip on wet output (prevents clipping to speakers)
- Lower defaults: decay 70→50, shimmer 50→30, damping 50→60, size 70→60
- Sync C++ defaults, TS constructor defaults, and factory defaults

Verified: peak output 0.5 at extreme params (decay=1, shimmer=1) — was
unbounded before. Effect still sounds lush at normal settings.

commit.message:
fix(frontend): Stop production localhost fallback #21 (#22)

* fix(frontend): Stop production localhost fallback #21

Prevent production builds from silently using http://localhost:8000 when VITE_API_URL is missing. Share API and WebSocket URL construction through a single helper and disable login with a clear configuration error until the deployment is configured correctly.

* fix(frontend): Fail Vercel builds without API URL #21

Make Vercel deployments stop immediately when VITE_API_URL is missing so the frontend cannot ship another production bundle that silently points at localhost.

* fix(frontend): Remove Vercel build-time API guard #21

Keep the production runtime safeguard against localhost redirects, but allow deployments without VITE_API_URL so Vercel can publish the build and show the explicit configuration error on /login.

* fix(frontend): Validate API URL configuration #21

Validate VITE_API_URL before using it, surface a broader backend configuration error for missing or malformed values, and URL-encode the WebSocket token query parameter.

* docs(deploy): Add backend deployment path #21

Add a Render blueprint for the FastAPI backend, document how to obtain the backend origin for VITE_API_URL, and make the runtime configuration error tell users exactly what to deploy and where to point the frontend. Also derive the Google callback URL from the current backend origin when no explicit override is set.

* docs(readme): Add Render deployment walkthrough #21

Add step-by-step instructions for deploying the backend on Render, wiring Google OAuth, and setting VITE_API_URL in Vercel so users unfamiliar with Render can bring the production stack online.

* fix(deploy): Tighten backend URL handling #21

Reject malformed VITE_API_URL values with query strings, fragments, or embedded credentials, enforce safer derived Google redirect URIs behind proxies, and enable uvicorn proxy header handling in the Render start command.

* fix(review): Remove WebSocket URL auth #21

Address review 4092774736 by quoting the Render env var value as a string, moving WebSocket authentication out of the URL and into an initial auth message, and updating the backend handshake and docs to match the safer protocol.

* fix(chat): Timeout WebSocket auth handshake #21

Close unauthenticated WebSocket connections if the initial auth message never arrives, preventing idle sockets from lingering indefinitely after accept().

* fix(backend/routers/chat.py): `await close_unauthorized_websocket(websocket)`

---------

# Code review fixes

- Validate `VITE_API_URL` before use and surface clearer backend configuration errors for missing or malformed values.
- Reject `VITE_API_URL` values with query strings, hash fragments, or embedded credentials, and normalize valid URLs before
concatenation.
- Harden production OAuth deployment behavior by enabling proxy-header support on Render and refusing non-HTTPS derived Google
callback URLs outside local development.
- Fix Render environment typing by setting `DEMO_ALLOW_SELF_ROLE_CHANGE` to the literal string "false".
- Remove JWTs from WebSocket URLs and switch WebSocket authentication to an initial auth message after connect.
- Add a timeout for the initial WebSocket auth handshake so idle unauthenticated sockets are closed instead of lingering.
- Update backend/docs/PR metadata to match the shipped WebSocket auth flow and deployment behavior.

---------

perf(bench): wrap benchmarks in functions to match V8 setup

All 9 benchmarks now wrap hot code in function bench() { ... } bench();
matching V8's benchmarks.js structure. This enables:
- Local variables → register allocation (vs global IC lookup)
- Maglev compilation for inner functions
- LICM, global promotion, and other optimizations

Previously benchmarks ran as top-level scripts where var declarations
become globals (LdaGlobal/StaGlobal ~10+ cycles each). Inside functions
they become local registers (Ldar/Star ~1 cycle each).

Expected impact: 10-100x improvement for far benchmarks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
